basic_details.job_title: Kerala CSEB Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 133 DEO, Junior Clerk and More Posts
basic_details.post_name: Secretary, Assistant Secretary / Chief Accountant, Junior Clerk - Super Grade Banks, Junior Clerk - Special Grade & Class-I Banks, Junior Clerk - Class-II to Class-VII Banks, System Administrator, Data Entry Operator (DEO)
basic_details.short_information: Kerala CSEB is recruiting 133 posts with B.Com, Diploma, M.Com, MBA/PGDM qualification, last date 30 June 2026.

important_dates.exam_date: To be announced after application closure

application_fee.details: All candidates must pay the examination fee along with 18% GST as per Government Order (S.O.(MS) No.17/2024/Co-op dated 13/08/2024). | Important: Candidates can apply to more than one bank/society in a single advertisement. Each additional institution requires an additional fee of ₹50. Applications by post are not accepted - online submission only.

vacancy_details.Post Name: Junior Clerk / Cashier - Super Grade Banks
Junior Clerk / Cashier - Special Grade Banks

vacancy_details.Bank Name: Thiruvananthapuram District Electricity Board Employees Co-op Society 3039

Padinharenhi Co-op Rural Bank F.1321, Kannur
Thrissur District Cooperative Employees Society R325
Multiple banks in Thrissur, Palakkad, Malappuram, Wayanad, Kannur
Reserve Bank of India Employees CS 3861
Eeratheepara Aided School Teachers CS K.746
Eranad Prathamika CS Karshika Gramavikasana Bank 4363
Kanjiramuzha Co-op Urban Credit Society P1172

exam_pattern.details: The CSEB written examination consists of 100 questions carrying 1 mark each (total 100 marks). | For each bank/society in the eligible list, candidates get a maximum of 20 marks allocated for the interview stage To qualify for the interview, a candidate must score a minimum of 4 marks in the written exam (out of the 20 marks allocated per institution) The actual interview cutoff is 16 marks out of 20 | Exam Mode: | Posts 15, 16, 20, 21: Online (Computer-Based) Examination Posts 17, 18, 19: OMR-Based (Offline) Examination

how_to_apply: Visit the official CSEB website: www.cseb.kerala.gov.in
New candidates: Complete One Time Registration (OTR) first. You can apply only after completing OTR.
Already registered candidates: Log in with your existing profile and proceed to apply online.
Select the advertisement number for the post you want to apply for.
Read the full notification carefully before submitting.
Fill in all required details accurately.
Pay the examination fee online (with 18% GST).
Submit the application and print a copy from the "My Application" section.
Note: Postal applications will not be accepted under any circumstances. Only online submissions through the CSEB portal are valid.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for Kerala CSEB Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for all 7 advertisements (Advt. No. 15/2026 to 21/2026) is June 30, 2026. Applications must be submitted through the CSEB portal at www.cseb.kerala.gov.in. Postal applications are not accepted.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are announced in Kerala CSEB May 2026 notifications?
faq.answer: CSEB Kerala has released 7 notifications with vacancies across multiple cooperative banks statewide. The posts include Secretary (3 vacancies across 3 banks), Assistant Secretary/Chief Accountant (2 vacancies), Junior Clerk across Super Grade, Special Grade/Class-I, and Class-II to VII banks (large number across dozens of banks), System Administrator (2 vacancies), and Data Entry Operator (4 vacancies).

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for Kerala CSEB 2026 recruitment?
faq.answer: The age limit, calculated as on January 1, 2026, is minimum 18 years and maximum 40 years. SC/ST candidates get 5 years relaxation, OBC/EWS get 3 years, Persons with Disability (40%+) get 5 years, and widows get 5 years relaxation.

faq.question: Q4. What is OTR (One Time Registration) in CSEB Kerala?
faq.answer: One Time Registration (OTR) is a one-time profile creation on the CSEB portal that is mandatory before applying for any CSEB advertisement. Once your OTR is complete, you can use those credentials to apply for any current or future CSEB advertisements without re-entering your basic details. However, completing OTR alone does not constitute an application - you must separately apply for each specific advertisement you wish to be considered for.

faq.question: Q5. What is the qualification required for Kerala cooperative bank Junior Clerk?
faq.answer: For Junior Clerk posts (Advt. 17, 18, 19), the minimum qualification is SSLC (or equivalent) AND the Personal Co-operative Training Course - Junior Diploma in Co-operation (JDC/NDC). The JDC is conducted by KSCU (Kerala State Co-operative Union) and is the mandatory basic cooperative qualification. Candidates who additionally hold a degree, B.Com with cooperation, or Cooperative Higher Diploma (HDC/HDC&BM) are also eligible.

faq.question: Q6. What is the exam fee for CSEB Kerala 2026?
faq.answer: For general category candidates, the fee is ₹150 for the first bank/society and ₹50 for each additional bank/society applied under the same advertisement. SC/ST candidates pay ₹50 for the first institution and ₹50 for each additional. All fees attract 18% GST as per the Government Order dated 13/08/2024. Fees must be paid online through the CSEB portal.

faq.question: Q7. Can I apply for multiple cooperative banks in the same CSEB advertisement?
faq.answer: Yes. One of the unique features of CSEB Kerala recruitment is that candidates can apply to multiple cooperative banks/societies within a single advertisement. You pay ₹150 (General) or ₹50 (SC/ST) for the first institution, and ₹50 extra for each additional institution. This significantly increases your chances since you are effectively competing for multiple vacancies across different banks in a single exam cycle.

faq.question: Q8. What is the CSEB Kerala written exam pattern?
faq.answer: The CSEB written examination consists of 100 questions carrying 1 mark each (total 100 marks). For Junior Clerk posts (Advt. 17, 18, 19), the exam is OMR-based (offline). For Secretary, Assistant Secretary, System Administrator, and DEO posts (Advt. 15, 16, 20, 21), it is an online computer-based examination. A minimum of 4 marks (out of the 20 marks allocated per bank for interview) is required for interview eligibility.
